title: How a request flows through a microservice
brief: Trace one request: client -> API gateway -> service -> cache/db -> response. One box per hop, arrows between them; a slide for the cache hit/miss path; takeaway on where latency hides.

title: How RAG works
brief: Retrieval-augmented generation end to end: user query -> embed -> vector search over your docs -> top-k chunks as context -> LLM -> grounded answer. One slide per stage; takeaway on why RAG beats fine-tuning for fresh/private knowledge.

title: The TLS handshake, visually
brief: ClientHello -> ServerHello + certificate -> key exchange -> Finished -> encrypted traffic. One slide per step with client/server boxes and arrows; takeaway on what's protected and what isn't.

title: Database indexing: why a B-tree is fast
brief: Full scan vs index lookup; a B-tree drawn as a few levels of nodes; how a lookup walks root -> branch -> leaf; takeaway on when an index helps and when it hurts writes.

title: The CAP theorem in plain diagrams
brief: Consistency, Availability, Partition tolerance as a triangle; what a network partition is; CP vs AP choices with one example each; takeaway that you only really choose between C and A during a partition.

title: OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow
brief: User -> app -> auth server -> consent -> code -> token exchange -> API call with access token. Boxes for user/app/auth-server/resource-server and labelled arrows; takeaway on why the code-exchange step exists.

title: Caching layers, from browser to database
brief: A request passing through browser cache -> CDN -> app cache (Redis) -> database; where each layer short-circuits; TTL and invalidation; takeaway on cache-aside as the default pattern.

title: Event-driven architecture with a message queue
brief: Producer -> queue -> consumer(s); decoupling and buffering of spikes; retries + dead-letter queue; takeaway on when async beats a synchronous call.

title: How Git branching and merging works
brief: Commits as a graph of nodes; a branch as a moving pointer; merge vs rebase shown as two small graphs; takeaway on keeping history readable.

title: Tokens, embeddings, and vectors
brief: Text -> tokens -> vectors in space; similar meaning = nearby vectors shown as dots on a plane; how cosine similarity powers search; takeaway connecting this to RAG and semantic search.

title: Rate limiting: token bucket vs leaky bucket
brief: A bucket filling with tokens vs draining at a fixed rate; how each shapes bursty traffic differently; takeaway on which to pick for APIs.

title: Load balancing strategies
brief: One load balancer fanning out to N servers; round-robin vs least-connections vs hashing shown simply; health checks; takeaway on sticky sessions and statelessness.

title: Prompting vs RAG vs fine-tuning
brief: Three columns comparing what each changes, cost, and when to use it; a simple decision arrow (start with prompting -> add RAG for knowledge -> fine-tune for behavior); takeaway as a one-line rule.

title: How the DNS lookup resolves a domain
brief: Browser -> resolver -> root -> TLD -> authoritative -> IP, then the actual request; caching at each hop; takeaway on why DNS changes take time to propagate.
